message:
docs: log 2026-04-22 full-review session and file 3 new fuzz-coverage beads

UPGRADE_LOG.md records the cc-fs full review session that landed the
test-suite fixes in the preceding commit (btrfs CRC stamping, asupersync
0.3 headroom, clippy too_many_lines allow). The log captures test/clippy
status (PASS + 6 vendored-fuser warnings; 4 pre-existing conformance
failures tracked in bd-uyjcz), the 17 fuzz-coverage gaps filed during
the `/testing-fuzzing` audit, and the known-issue ledger (bd-uyjcz for
conformance, bd-scqdb for the flaky dispatch_ioctl_move_ext rejection
test), plus a saturation note from cod-fs confirming `ffs-core` parser
fuzz frontier is idle beyond the existing backlog.

.beads/issues.jsonl gains three new P2 fuzz-target beads surfaced during
the audit:

- bd-11yv0 — fuzz_lrc_repair for ffs_repair::lrc repair_local_single /
  repair_global erasure-code paths (corrupt-block injection).
- bd-1dkja — fuzz_xattr_parsing for ffs_xattr::parse_xattr_name /
  list_xattrs (string/byte xattr name + listing).
- bd-9zg6j — fuzz_inode_roundtrip for ffs_inode::serialize_inode and
  encode_extra_timestamp (parse → serialize → equality).

All three are tracked for later delivery; the log section lists them
alongside the 14 other fuzz beads already filed in the same session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-anfg0","title":"Expand ext4 dir block checksum tests beyond round-trip + no-panic","description":"crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs has stamp/verify_dir_block_checksum but the proptest coverage in the same file is limited to:

  - ext4_proptest_verify_dir_block_checksum_no_panic (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_verify_dir_block_checksum_short_rejects (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_stamp_verify (round-trip)

None of these exercise the per-inode seed mixing — i.e. the kernel's defence against:
  - replaying a valid dir block under a different (ino, generation) tuple
  - using a stamp produced under one filesystem csum_seed on another filesystem instance
  - flipping a bit in the directory entry payload after a valid stamp

Apply the same four metamorphic relations the inode checksum harness now covers:
  - MR-INO: identical body, two different ino numbers → different stored checksums
  - MR-GEN: identical body+ino, different generation → different stored checksums
  - MR-SEED: identical body+ino+gen, different csum_seed → different stored checksums
  - MR-DETECT: single-bit flip in the covered region [0..bs-12] invalidates verification

Land them as proptests in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs alongside the existing ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_stamp_verify.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-frankenfs-pane","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:12:22.264599421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:18:57.022680334Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T22:18:57.020390690Z","close_reason":"Added MR-INO, MR-GEN, MR-SEED, MR-DETECT proptests for dir block checksum, mirroring the inode-checksum template from bd-z75f6.  {"id":"bd-aw9l8","title":"[MEDIUM] vendored fuser dead-code warnings are leaking into workspace gates","description":"# Review finding

Both workspace gates emit warnings from the vendored `fuser` dependency before project crates run. The current run surfaced dead-code warnings in:
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/fuse_abi.rs`
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/request.rs`

## Warnings seen in current run
- `cuse_init_in` is never constructed
- `cuse_init_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_ioctl_iovec` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_in` is never constructed
- method `unrestricted` is never used

## Why this matters
These are not the current hard stop because `ffs-tui` fails clippy first, but they still pollute the mandated workspace `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` output. Once the `ffs-tui` lifetime issue is fixed, this vendored warning inventory may become the next gate blocker under `-D warnings`.

## Repro
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

 {"id":"bd-h9lpq","title":"[HIGH] 16 KiB btrfs readdir fixture bypasses the shared checksum helper and drifts from parser semantics","description":"# Review finding

The remaining red btrfs directory-walk family is the only synthetic-tree fixture in `ffs-core` still hand-rolling tree-block checksum logic instead of going through the shared test helper.

## Evidence
- Shared helper exists at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22328-22332`: `stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c(image, logical)`.
- That helper is used by the green 4 KiB synthetic-tree fixtures (`crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22357-22561`, `23010-23011`).
- `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` instead inlines its own checksum math at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23372-23375` while also switching the superblock `nodesize` to `16384`.
- The current full workspace gate still fails the six readdir-specific tests on `tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)`, which means the hand-rolled 16 KiB path has drifted from what the parser expects.

## Why this is high severity
This is now the only remaining workspace-red btrfs fixture family after the default-root smoke fix landed. Keeping a second checksum implementation in test builders is actively hiding the real contract and slowing down recovery.

## Expected fix direction
Generalize the shared test checksum helper to accept nodesize/logical span explicitly, then route `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` through that helper and add a narrow regression test that proves a 16 KiB stamped leaf round-trips through the current parser.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
